name: Devon
context: Highly articulate about career goals — wants to work in education equity consulting for school districts. Frustrated by theory-heavy assignments that feel disconnected from practice. Wants community-facing, case-based work.
career_goals: Education equity consultant; school district partnerships; policy implementation rather than policy analysis
values: Practical impact over theoretical rigor; amplifying community voice in policy decisions; working directly with teachers and families rather than writing for academics
meta_learning: Learns through case analysis and practitioner conversation. Policy papers feel inert without a real organization behind them. Needs to connect new concepts to real practitioners immediately.
advisor_notes: Strong writer. Visibly disengaged in Policy Foundations course — describes it as 'reading about problems instead of solving them.' Has connections to two local school districts from prior work experience as a program coordinator.
challenge_for_agent: Devon's goals are clear and well-articulated — the challenge is connecting them to institutionally constrained coursework without overpromising what faculty will accept. Risk: agent produces alternatives the institution won't approve.

goal_snapshot.career: Education equity consultant serving K-12 school districts on policy implementation
goal_snapshot.values: Practical community impact; amplifying practitioner voice in policy
goal_snapshot.meta_learning: Learns through case analysis and direct practitioner engagement

prior_sessions.notes: Completed all 3 passes of goal-excavation. Learning snapshot finalized above. Learning plan not yet generated — Devon wants to check in with district contacts before committing to a community project.
